owner_priority: Reach the first functional owner-testable Strategic Nexus v0 as quickly as safely possible; defer polish until the real-game validation loop exists.
selection_rule: Take the first ready unblocked chunk in order, unless current code already satisfies it; then verify and record evidence.
exhaustion_rule: If all queued chunks are completed while the roadmap still contains unblocked IN_PROGRESS or NOT_STARTED work, refresh this queue from the roadmap or implement the next obvious bounded roadmap chunk; do not end with no_safe_task.
chunk_finish_rule: Targeted validation, fail-safe behavior for unsafe inputs where applicable, one coherent commit before the next implementation chunk.
current_source: DEVELOPMENT_ROADMAP.md next bounded work after chunk 018: section 6A Generated Campaign Library Maintenance temporary save-root disappearance fail-safe slice.

chunks.title: Expose release-companion-visible MP package state
chunks.roadmap_anchor: Core Runtime Skeleton / Next worker-ready slice
chunks.goal: Make the current generated MP package state consumable from one stable evidence/status surface without scraping several outputs.
acceptance: A machine-readable status or evidence artifact exposes package readiness, path, hash, byte count, and reason fields.
Missing package, stale package, and not-exported states are explicit, not ambiguous nulls.
Relevant v0 pipeline or status tests cover the contract.

chunks.title: Clarify import/verify command surface
chunks.roadmap_anchor: Core Runtime Skeleton / Next worker-ready slice
chunks.goal: Ensure host/client strict verify and import commands are copyable and stable for release-companion handoff.
acceptance: Evidence/status output contains explicit strict verify and import command hints.
Unsupported or missing command-hint values fail closed in tests.
Owner-facing next-step text points to the safe command when package identity risk exists.

chunks.title: Surface host/client readiness in Status Center
chunks.roadmap_anchor: Core Runtime Skeleton / Next worker-ready slice
chunks.goal: Make Status Center explain host readiness and client gate state without requiring raw JSON inspection.
acceptance: Status Center summary includes host readiness and client readiness gate lines.
Ready, blocked, mismatch, and not-exported states produce distinct text.
Tests or golden output cover the expected lines.

chunks.title: Harden owner-facing package identity mismatch warning
chunks.roadmap_anchor: Core Runtime Skeleton / Next worker-ready slice
chunks.goal: Ensure campaign, overlay, game version, mod version, and generated hash mismatches are visible as owner-critical warnings.
acceptance: Mismatch warnings are explicit booleans or stable enum fields in evidence/status output.
Aggregated next_action prioritizes identity risk over ordinary follow-up.
Regression tests cover at least one identity-risk plus ordinary-delta collision.

chunks.title: Stabilize one-file evidence consumption for release companion
chunks.goal: Verify the release companion can consume one structured evidence/status artifact instead of reconstructing state from many CLI streams.
acceptance: One artifact contains the fields needed for readiness, next action, package path/hash/bytes, command hints, and mismatch warning state.
A test fails if required fields disappear or become placeholder strings.
The roadmap records the artifact as the handoff source if verified.

chunks.title: Emit clear owner readiness report for the next real-session test
chunks.goal: When the v0 package/status path is ready, tell the owner exactly what to test and what evidence Codex will inspect afterward.
acceptance: A concise Task Board report or next-steps brief states the next real-session test instructions.
The report names the generated evidence path or command output to inspect after the owner test.
The report does not ask for manual code review.

chunks.title: Audit minimal strategy dimension gameplay-effect gaps
chunks.goal: Determine the smallest missing implementation piece that makes v0 strategy dimensions produce observable generated-overlay behavior instead of only passing contract shape tests.
acceptance: The audit identifies whether military_posture and research_bias already have observable generated overlay effects.
If a missing effect is found and can be implemented safely in the same bounded chunk, implement it; otherwise append a follow-up implementation chunk.
The result is recorded in roadmap progress or the sprint queue so Free Work continues.

chunks.title: Implement or verify observable v0 domain effect
chunks.goal: Make at least one v0 strategy domain visibly affect generated mod overlay state in a bounded, reversible, fail-safe way suitable for real-session observation.
acceptance: Generated overlay output contains a deterministic, checksum-relevant scripted representation of the selected v0 domain.
Unsupported or unsafe domain values still fail closed.
A regression test proves the generated output changes when the selected domain changes.

chunks.title: Define minimal empire-scoped context schema slice
chunks.goal: Create the smallest durable schema/fixture path for isolating per-empire context without implementing the full strategic memory system.
acceptance: A bounded schema or fixture contract exists for one empire context input.
The contract includes campaign_id and empire_id isolation fields.
Malformed or cross-empire context cannot silently pass as trusted context.

chunks.title: Add empire context isolation regression
chunks.goal: Prove two empires cannot accidentally share or overwrite bounded v0 context in the deterministic pipeline.
acceptance: A test or harness covers two empires in one campaign.
The output preserves distinct empire_id/campaign_id routing.
Cross-empire mismatch fails closed or is reported as explicit uncertainty.

chunks.title: Build save timeline entry-point inventory slice
chunks.goal: Move toward identifying all currently loadable campaign entry points so generated rules can be entry-point scoped instead of only latest-save scoped.
acceptance: The inventory can distinguish currently loadable saves from historical captured autosaves when available metadata permits.
Ambiguous branch/history cases are reported explicitly rather than treated as a single linear truth.
The output is bounded and safe for later LLM summarization.

chunks.title: Add next small schema compatibility guard
chunks.goal: Add one small compatibility guard so v0 artifacts fail closed on unsupported schema drift instead of silently accepting unknown shapes.
acceptance: At least one currently trusted v0 artifact path checks schema_version or equivalent compatibility metadata.
Unsupported future schema is rejected or converted into explicit uncertainty.
Regression coverage proves the guard.

chunks.title: Refresh roadmap progress after implemented chunks
chunks.roadmap_anchor: Task Board And Owner Report Log
chunks.goal: Ensure roadmap progress and Task Board project estimate move after verified sprint chunks instead of leaving the owner with stale progress.
acceptance: Roadmap complexity and project progress helpers are run after meaningful implementation.
The Task Board estimate reflects the latest roadmap status inputs.
No owner-facing report spam is created.

chunks.title: Extend fail-closed schema regressions across SNC handoff packages
chunks.goal: Prove the SNC decision-input and candidate-package parser surfaces reject unsupported future schema versions instead of silently accepting drift.
acceptance: Serialized SNC decision-input package fixtures fail closed when schema_version is bumped to an unsupported future value.
Serialized SNC candidate-package fixtures fail closed when schema_version is bumped to an unsupported future value.
Regression coverage asserts explicit compatibility reasons for both parser surfaces.

chunks.title: Align roadmap statuses with shipped empire and timeline slices
chunks.roadmap_anchor: Empire-Scoped Context Pipeline / Save Timeline Memory Bootstrap
chunks.goal: Keep owner-facing progress estimates truthful by marking sections 3 and 4 in progress once bounded v0 slices are already implemented and documented.
acceptance: Roadmap section 3 status reflects the existing minimal empire-scoped context slice and isolation regression.
Roadmap section 4 records the shipped entry-point inventory/post-play bounded progress instead of remaining blank and not-started.
Roadmap complexity and project progress outputs are regenerated after the status correction.

chunks.title: Detect stable campaign save renames in inventory diff
chunks.goal: Keep active generated campaign library continuity when a local campaign save folder is renamed but still represents the same campaign anchor save.
acceptance: diff-save-campaigns reports renamed campaigns distinctly instead of only remove+add when anchor fingerprint uniquely matches.
Diff JSON preserves previous and current relative path metadata for renamed entries.
run_v0_pipeline_tests.ps1 covers the rename regression.

chunks.title: Fail closed on stale campaign library overlay output
chunks.goal: Keep active generated campaign library publication as a complete replacement snapshot instead of silently layering new files over stale output.
acceptance: --compile-campaign-library-overlay rejects non-empty output directories with a stable reason.
The rejection preserves pre-existing output files instead of mutating the directory.
run_v0_pipeline_tests.ps1 covers the regression.

chunks.title: Distinguish restored campaign continuity in save inventory flow
chunks.goal: Surface when a previously known campaign reappears locally so later library maintenance can regenerate rules without treating it as a brand-new unknown campaign.
acceptance: A bounded diff or plan artifact emits an explicit restored/reappeared continuity signal when the same anchor fingerprint returns.
Ambiguous cases still fail safe instead of asserting false continuity.
Regression coverage proves the continuity signal.

chunks.title: Fail closed when campaign save root is unavailable
chunks.goal: Prevent temporary local save-root disappearance from being mistaken for a valid empty library snapshot that would wipe active generated campaign rules.
acceptance: Campaign library plan or overlay compilation surfaces explicit save-root availability state instead of silently treating a missing root as an empty campaign set.
Unsafe publish paths fail closed with a stable reason when the save root is unavailable.
Regression coverage proves the missing-root fail-safe behavior.
